title: Find the number of digits

content.problemStatement: Given an integer **N**, Calculate and print the number of digits present in **N**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of decimal digits in N.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The number 123456789 consists of nine digits.

solution.approach: The number of digits of a positive integer N is simply the length of its decimal representation. We can obtain it by converting N to a string and taking its length, or by repeatedly dividing N by 10 and counting how many times we can do this before N becomes zero. Both methods run in O(number of digits) time, which is at most 19 for N ≤ 10^18, and use O(1) extra space. The string method is concise and avoids overflow concerns, while the division method works with numeric types directly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(D) per test case, where D is the number of digits of N (≤ 19). Overall O(T·D).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input and output buffers.
